result:
I’d abolish the neurological roots of cruelty—erase the conditions in which one mind’s pain looks, feels, or sounds irrelevant to another mind.

Not to make everyone agree, or sit in perfect harmony, but so that when interests collide the conflict still hurts. No one worth dismissing. No horseplay lobotomy.

Call it an empathetic horizon: every choice is automatically cross-checked against the full, undiminished raw interior of everyone it would touch. No dark corners in which indifference hides; no distance where the screams lessen.

We would still feel rage, still compete, still err. But no one would reach to conquer, torture, impoverish, or exile without experiencing the price as personally as the benefit they sought.

That single edit would let every other cruelty you can name—war, slavery, poverty—collapse under its own distorted weight.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
